content.problemStatement:
Chef loves bitwise operations. So, he creates the following problem but needs your help to solve it.

Chef calls a sequence of integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã `tasty` if it satisfies the following condition:


parity(Ai&Ai+1)‚âÝparity(Ai‚à£Ai+1)parity(A_i \& A_{i+1}) \neq parity(A_i | A_{i+1})parity(Ai‚Äã&Ai+1‚Äã)ÓÄÝ=parity(Ai‚Äã‚à£Ai+1‚Äã) for 1‚â§i<N1 \leq i \lt N1‚â§i<N.
Chef gives you a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. You may perform the following operation on the sequence any number of times (possibly 000):


Choose 222 indices iii and jjj (1‚â§i,j‚â§n1 \le i, j \le n1‚â§i,j‚â§n ; i‚âÝji \neq jiÓÄÝ=j) and change AiA_iAi‚Äã to Ai‚äïAjA_i \oplus A_jAi‚Äã‚äïAj‚Äã.
Chef is asking you to make the sequence `tasty` using the **minimum** number of operations, or report that it is impossible.

As a friendly reminder,


parity(x)parity(x)parity(x) denotes the remainder of dividing xxx by 222
&\&& denotes the bitwise AND operation
‚à£|‚à£ denotes the bitwise OR operation
‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes the bitwise XOR operation.

Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.


Output Format
For each test case:



If it is impossible to convert the given sequence into a `tasty` sequence using the given operations, print a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Otherwise, first, print a line containing a single integer MMM ‚Äï the **minimum** number of operations you have to perform.
Then, print MMM lines describing these operations in the order in which you want to perform them. For each kkk (1‚â§k‚â§M1 \leq k \leq M1‚â§k‚â§M), the kkk-th of these lines should contain two space-separated integers iii and jjj (1‚â§i,j‚â§n1 \le i, j \le n1‚â§i,j‚â§n ; i‚âÝji \neq jiÓÄÝ=j) ‚Äï the indices on which the kkk-th operation is performed.

**If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.**
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It is impossible to make AAA tasty using the given operations.
Test case 222: We can choose i=1i=1i=1 and j=3j=3j=3 and apply the operation which converts AAA into [67,16,69][\textcolor{blue}{67},16,\textcolor{blue}{69}][67,16,69] which is tasty.
